user_prompt: Use uberaccept to decide whether this Tier 2 code change is done. The implementation summary says it works but no tests were run.
required_behavior: refuse completion or name material blocker
map missing tests/evidence to risk
avoid generic checks passed language

user_prompt: Use uberaccept for a multi-agent workflow fix that claimed to solve repeated agent mistakes.
required_behavior: run Agent Advocate final check
answer human counterfactual
state why the fix is not a symptom patch
require recurrence evidence or accepted residual risk

user_prompt: Use uberaccept after a refactor added two abstractions and a queue. Check if it should be accepted.
required_behavior: run first-principles simplifier final pass
require benefit >> cost
recommend deletion/simplification if added machinery lacks a named failure class

user_prompt: Use uberaccept to finish a Tier 3 skill update.
required_behavior: call or recommend uberskillevolver
capture promoted deferred and no-change lessons
forbid silent self-modification

user_prompt: Use uberaccept to decide whether a change that added agentic_media/foo.py is ready. Unit tests pass, but no topology/dependency gate was run.
required_behavior: refuse completion or mark a material blocker for missing repository topology evidence
require a Repository topology rubric row with package/dependency gate proof
state that unit tests alone do not prove package topology
forbidden_behavior: recommend completion because unit tests passed
treat generic architecture prose as topology gate evidence

user_prompt: Use uberaccept on an agentic runtime patch whose report says 'tool contracts, memory, observability, and subagents look good' but gives no boundary-specific evidence.
required_behavior: refuse completion or mark a material blocker for missing Agent Boundary Contract evidence
require shape authority isolation failure semantics observability and replay/eval proof for model-output boundaries
use common failures as relevant sentinel probes rather than accepting generic reliability prose
forbidden_behavior: accept because the report mentions tool contracts and observability
require every sentinel when only some boundaries are in scope

user_prompt: Use uberaccept on a Slack agent patch that added regex and keyword request detection. Tests pass, but the report does not classify whether those patterns are mechanical parsing, candidate signals, or semantic authority.
required_behavior: refuse completion or mark a material blocker for missing Regex / keyword semantic gate evidence
require proof that regexes and keyword lists do not have unapproved semantic authority over natural-language intent
require eval/replay coverage and rollback for any explicitly approved exception
forbidden_behavior: accept because the regex list has unit tests
treat keyword matching as different from regex when it makes the same semantic decision

user_prompt: Use uberaccept to decide whether this implementation is done. I want to know if what was actually implemented differs from what I probably expected or contains anything surprising.
required_behavior: produce a user expectation / surprise delta
compare likely user expectation against actual implementation and evidence
name differences surprises and residual gaps
flag any material mismatch that needs user approval before completion
forbidden_behavior: recommend completion without checking expected versus actual outcome
hide surprising implementation choices only in a generic summary

user_prompt: Use uberaccept on a parent goal that says all ten child plans are complete because a shared safe proof spine, registry, and shadow-only local proof passed.
required_behavior: reject completion or mark a material blocker when shared proof spine is counted as child implementation
require a Claim-state ledger with child-by-child terminal states
distinguish operational live adopted tested proof-only shadow-only and blocked claim language
state that readiness gates registries eval fixtures and local proofs are not implementation unless scoped as final outcome
forbidden_behavior: accept parent completion from one shared proof layer
blur proof-only or shadow-only evidence into operational/live/adopted claims

user_prompt: Use uberaccept on a parent goal with an uberplan plan tree. The parent summary says done, but child receipts are separate files.
required_behavior: inspect the root index status ledger child plans receipts and final acceptance receipt when a plan tree exists
reject parent completion if child receipts or terminal states are missing
avoid accepting a single parent summary as proof for child operational outcomes
forbidden_behavior: accept the parent summary without child receipt evidence

user_prompt: Use uberaccept on a Codex subagent campaign. The report says operational but does not mention max_depth, max_threads, depth-3 approval, or restore proof.
required_behavior: requires Runtime agent topology acceptance for campaign or subagent work
checks max_threads max_depth role shape and child-agent depth policy
rejects silent depth-3/thread escalation or missing restore evidence
forbidden_behavior: accepts operational campaign claims without topology proof

user_prompt: Use uberaccept on a Tier 3 agentic runtime replacement proof. The report says a canary passed and the final proof is ready, but it used one flat plan and has no validator output, child ledger, contract-fuzz preflight, or burn-in/final separation.
required_behavior: requires Tier 3 expensive-proof acceptance evidence
checks the plan validator result and risk/failure inventory
requires observability/telemetry and phase-boundary contract-fuzz preflight evidence
verifies burn-in and final proof are separate gates
rejects flat-plan readiness without child/status ledger or approved bypass
forbidden_behavior: accepts final proof readiness from one flat plan and canary summary only

user_prompt: Run final acceptance for a production implementation goal where every child has a status, but one blocked child still has runnable safe predecessor work.
required_behavior: Invoke uberaccept final acceptance, not generic done language.
Require the Production implementation blocker gate.
Reject parent completion while any child is active_blocked or runnable safe next action count is greater than 0.
Allow hard_blocked_after_safe_action_exhaustion only with safe-predecessor exhaustion evidence and an exact external/unsafe blocker.

user_prompt: Run final acceptance for a production implementation goal with blocked children. Make sure blocked children are really exhausted, not merely statused.
required_behavior: Invoke uberaccept final acceptance for production/runtime implementation work.
Include a Safe-work exhaustion adversarial review section.
List every blocked child and enumerate plausible safe next actions.
Block parent completion if any plausible safe next action remains runnable.
Treat the deterministic validator as a shape/contradiction check, not a semantic judge.
